title: Whats the laptop screen in EoEs opening Misato scene about
display_title: Whats the laptop screen in EoEs opening Misato scene about
wikitext:
[]: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by Chainer3000 » Tue Aug 09, 2016 8:57 am Sorry if this has been covered - i've searched just about all the places I could think of and never found an answer... While rewatching again, I remembered it was a lingering question. What's the laptop screen in EoE's Misato opening scene about? She says something to the effect of, "so this is the truth about the second impact" - Is this a reference to something? It seems like gibberish in some places but it talks about anime and otaku in others...? It doesn't seem like it actually has anything to do with Eva http://imgur.com/YaBnXfy

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Tue Aug 09, 2016 9:00 am The laptop screen is a history of Gainax from what I remember. Just a fun little easter egg that has been thrown in there I'd imagine.

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 09, 2016 10:46 am jcmoorehead wrote: The laptop screen is a history of Gainax from what I remember.

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by Seven Fifty » Tue Aug 09, 2016 11:29 am The Eva Monkey wrote: It is indeed, although a bunch of names were changed from names of Gainax people to Eva terms to make it a bit less obvious.

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Aug 09, 2016 6:50 pm Didn't they slip that same text into Episode 21 as the Second Impact report?

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Aug 19, 2016 3:19 am One thing that kind of bugs me about this wonderful film is that it wants us to ignore the English text on Misato's laptop, but the English text on Maya's laptop is thematically important.

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by diwo » Mon Nov 14, 2016 3:01 pm Here is a better version of the picture if anyone is interested

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by StrokeMeGoat » Wed Nov 16, 2016 9:55 pm Tumbling Down wrote: One thing that kind of bugs me about this wonderful film is that it wants us to ignore the English text on Misato's laptop, but the English text on Maya's laptop is thematically important.

[]: The binary code             Quote Post by invisaboi72 » Sat Nov 06, 2021 11:08 pm The Binary goes as follows I hope someone can find a deeper meaning in it if there is one 0001001011 0111010100 1011011010 1101110100 0110100101 0010111010 1000101110 1011011001 1011010110 1010001110 1101110101 0000100011 0011010110 1101000100 0001000110 1001010011 1101010001 1010001000 4010001010 1101000000 1101011010 1101010100 1101010000 0010100101 0110111101 0010111010 1000101110 1011011001 1011010110 1010001110 1101110101 0000100011 0011010110 1101000100 0001000110 (It looks like a 4 on the screen It could be a 0 though) https://www.google.com/imgres?imgurl=ht ... gAegQIARAx Merged the thread "The binary code" into here, for better context. The other two posts of yours has been deleted: since they refer to the same subject - JoelcrNeto

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Sun Nov 07, 2021 10:07 am They look like random values to me: to have a (coherent) message we need the binary digits to be grouped into 7/8 bits. We need 7/8 bits to get 1 byte, which is the size of a character, but in that screen they are grouped into 10 bits. Also, some characters need to be grouped into 16 bits (2 bytes), such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean (CJK) characters -- Edit: I'm meant here about CJK characters in Unicode, sorry for confusion. I developed a quick JavaScript tool to decode the binaries, but it returned “weird” characters: Unicode characters - 10 bits wrote: Kǔ˚ʹƥȮ˙˖ʎ#Ö̈́F͑ʈ͔̀͐¥ƽȮ˙˖ʎ#Ö̈́F

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Nov 07, 2021 6:46 pm Remember back in 1995 Unicode was not yet widely used. For Japanese you'd need to be looking at things like [shift-]JIS, EUC, and EBCDIC as well as the ASCII-based options and Unicode you've doubtless been through.

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by AWinters » Fri Nov 12, 2021 6:12 pm This was done in episode 21 too. Maybe there's fuller context between the two. https://forum.evageeks.org/thread/20875/Episode-21-Third-Impact-papers/

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by OutlawThirds » Fri Nov 26, 2021 10:26 pm Re: Binary I tried it as Ascii and it's gibberish: ÝKktiK¨ºÙµ¨íÔ#5´ASÔh+@ÖµM@¥oK¨ºÙµ¨íÔ#5´A However, upon reflection, ASCII or Unicode might not be the right encodings to look for. This was the 90s and the creators might have used a character set like JIS or Shift-JIS. I don't have converters handy for those formats, but I'll do some more googling.

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by OutlawThirds » Tue Dec 14, 2021 3:13 pm Ok, here it is in ShiftJIS: Kﾔﾚt･ｺ.ﾙﾖ志#ﾖDFSQ�@ZTP･ｽｺ.ﾙﾖ志#ﾖDF ISO Japanese seems to be totally busted: K��t��.���u#�DFSQ��@ZTP���.���u#�DF EUC-JP: K壓tズ.掎�u#�DFSQ��@ZTPソ�.掎�u#�DF UTF 16 Big Endian at least resulted in mostly printable characters: 䯔�ꖺ⻙횎産홄䙓冈詀婔傥붺⻙횎産홄� I couldn't tell ya if any of that remotely makes sense. Here's the converter I used: https://www.rapidtables.com/convert/num ... ascii.html The presence of 4 makes me think that this wasn't mechanically converted from anything, but rather the result of random typing on a numberpad, where the 4 is above the 1 and it's easy for your finger to slip. The groupings of ten instead of eight is also suspect, I'll have to see if any text encoding scheme uses ten bits.

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Dec 14, 2021 6:15 pm I put that string of characters into a few machine translation tools; most of them don't recognize it at all in any language, and occasionally you just get some complete gibberish. deepL auto-recognizes it as Japanese, but doesn't actually translate it as anything until you manually pick Chinese, at which point it gives you this; The hem on the bottom of the hem is a hem on the bottom of the hem. So yeah, with all current leads we have, it appears to just be some random 8 bit sequences for aesthetics; that being said however, I could see it maybe being image encoding or something else that isn't text-specific

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Tue Dec 14, 2021 6:39 pm No surprises. Like I mentioned before: that binarie codes are 10-bit based , thus none of these character tables will return anything coherent -- so I had the stupid idea to try Unicode before. And even then I'm convinced that they're random values, merely aesthetic, precisely because of the incorrectly inserted 4.

[]: Re: What's the laptop screen in EoE's opening Misato scene about?             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Dec 14, 2021 10:51 pm Probably, yeah. 4 is right above 1 on the numpad, so there's a good chance that it's just a typo while creating some arbitrary numbers
